seoTitle: Record Reimbursable Expenses in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Track and invoice billable client expenses in QuickBooks Online by enabling billable tracking and passing costs onto customer invoices.
intro: Covering a client's materials or travel costs upfront only works if you actually pass those costs back onto their invoice. This guide shows how to enable billable expense tracking in QuickBooks Online and turn logged costs into client charges.

steps.heading: Enable billable expense tracking
steps.body: Go to Account and Settings, open the Expenses tab, and toggle on Make expenses and items billable.

steps.heading: Record the upfront expense
steps.body: Click + New, select Expense, choose your merchant, and pick the source bank or credit card account.

steps.heading: Mark it as billable
steps.body: Enter the category and cost, then check the newly visible Billable box and select the target customer.

steps.heading: Save the expense
steps.body: Review the details and click Save and Close to log the upfront cost against that client.

steps.heading: Invoice the client
steps.body: Create a new Invoice for that customer, and pull the pending billable expense into the invoice from the Add to Invoice sidebar tray.

faq.question: Do I need to turn on a setting before I can bill expenses to clients?
faq.answer: Yes, you first need to enable Make expenses and items billable under Account and Settings in the Expenses tab.

faq.question: How does QuickBooks know which client to bill?
faq.answer: When you check the Billable box on an expense, you also select the specific customer it should be tied to.

faq.question: Can I add a billable expense to an invoice after the fact?
faq.answer: Yes, when creating an invoice for that customer, pending billable expenses appear in a sidebar tray you can add with one click.

faq.question: How do I check my reimbursable expense margins?
faq.answer: Run the Profit and Loss by Customer report to verify that billed expenses balance out against the original costs.
